title: Fuertesimalva insularis
intro: Fuertesimalva insularis, also known as the Insular Small-flowered Mallow, is a shrub-like plant species native to the Galapagos Islands. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in wound healing, reducing inflammation, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Fuertesimalva insularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including skin irritations and minor injuries.
taxonomy: Fuertesimalva insularis is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Malpighiaceae. This family consists of around 130 genera of plants, commonly known as the barber's chair or malpighia family. The Malpighiaceae family is part of the larger order Malpighiales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Erythroxylaceae and Ochnaceae. Malpighiales is a part of the larger clade Rosidae, which includes many diverse groups of flowering plants. The exact classification of Fuertesimalva insularis may also be influenced by its geographical distribution and other factors.
